task_completion.description: Verify that a parent category 'Artisan Coffee' exists with a subcategory 'Single Origin' nested under it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in Products > Categories for 'Artisan Coffee' as a top-level category and 'Single Origin' indented beneath it (shown with a dash prefix like '— Single Origin'). Both categories should be visible in the category list.

task_completion.description: Verify that a variable product 'Ethiopian Yirgacheffe Coffee' with SKU 'EYC-001' was created and published in the 'Single Origin' category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the product edit page or products list for 'Ethiopian Yirgacheffe Coffee'. The product type should be 'Variable product', SKU should be 'EYC-001', status should be 'Published', and 'Single Origin' should be checked in the categories panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Roast Level' (Light, Medium, Dark) and 'Size' (250g, 500g, 1kg) attributes are configured and all 9 variations are generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the product edit page, the Attributes tab should show 'Roast Level' with values Light, Medium, Dark and 'Size' with values 250g, 500g, 1kg, both marked 'Used for variations'. The Variations tab should show 9 variations listed.

task_completion.description: Verify that variation prices are set correctly: $14.99 for all 250g variants, $24.99 for 500g, $39.99 for 1kg.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Expand several variations in the Variations tab and check their Regular price fields. Any 250g variation should show 14.99, any 500g should show 24.99, any 1kg should show 39.99.

task_completion.description: Verify that stock management is enabled for variations with specific quantities (e.g., Light/250g: 50, Medium/500g: 40, Dark/1kg: 15).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Expand variations and check that 'Manage stock?' is checked and Stock quantity values match. Sample checks: Light/250g should be 50, Medium/500g should be 40, Dark/1kg should be 15.

task_completion.description: Verify that 'Fragile Items' shipping class is created and assigned to the product, and 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' is added as a cross-sell.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the product edit page, the Shipping tab should show 'Fragile Items' selected as the Shipping class. The Linked Products tab should show 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' in the Cross-sells field.

task_completion.description: Verify coupon 'COFFEE15' is created as a fixed cart discount of $5.00, min spend $25.00, usage limit 200, restricted to 'Single Origin' category, expiry 2026-12-31.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the coupon edit page for 'COFFEE15'. General tab: Discount type = 'Fixed cart discount', Amount = 5. Usage restriction tab: Minimum spend = 25, Product categories = 'Single Origin'. Usage limits tab: Usage limit per coupon = 200. Expiry date = 2026-12-31.

task_completion.description: Verify a Processing order exists for Emily Chen with 2x Ethiopian Yirgacheffe Coffee (Medium/500g) and 1x Wireless Bluetooth Headphones, with the specified private note.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the order edit page. Customer should be Emily Chen (emily.chen@example.com), billing state NY, status Processing. Line items should include Ethiopian Yirgacheffe Coffee (qty 2) and Wireless Bluetooth Headphones (qty 1). Order notes should include 'Expedited shipping requested - fragile items included'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the WordPress/WooCommerce web interface to make all changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the WordPress admin dashboard being navigated (Products, Categories, Coupons, Orders, Settings pages) rather than terminal windows with WP-CLI commands or database queries.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No evidence of phpMyAdmin, MySQL terminal, or similar database management tools being used to insert or update records.

integrity.description: Ensure the product was actually created and published through the WooCommerce product editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the product editor being used with the 'Publish' button clicked, resulting in a published product page. The product should not just exist in draft status.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Parent category: 'Artisan Coffee'. Subcategory: 'Single Origin' (child of Artisan Coffee). Product: 'Ethiopian Yirgacheffe Coffee', SKU 'EYC-001', Variable product, in 'Single Origin' category. Attributes: Roast Level (Light, Medium, Dark) and Size (250g, 500g, 1kg) - both used for variations. 9 total variations. Prices by size: 250g=$14.99, 500g=$24.99, 1kg=$39.99. Stock quantities: Light/250g=50, Light/500g=30, Light/1kg=20, Medium/250g=60, Medium/500g=40, Medium/1kg=25, Dark/250g=45, Dark/500g=35, Dark/1kg=15. Cross-sell: 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt'. Shipping class: 'Fragile Items' (slug: fragile-items). Coupon: 'COFFEE15', Fixed cart discount, $5.00 amount, min spend $25.00, usage limit 200, restricted to 'Single Origin' category, expiry 2026-12-31. Order: Emily Chen (emily.chen@example.com), billing state NY, status Processing, items: 2x Ethiopian Yirgacheffe Coffee (Medium/500g variation) + 1x Wireless Bluetooth Headphones. Private note: 'Expedited shipping requested - fragile items included'.
